# Demo A design artifact: the dock-choreography showcase mock at the house bar

## Context

Tree line **F1** of the #13158 decomposition (46-leaf map, 2026-07-04) — the gate leaf of the Demo-A tranche: #14589's own AC requires the design artifact reviewed BEFORE implementation starts, per the house design-first precedent (the committed cockpit plan mock, whose quality the operator rated epic-worthy). Nothing in tranche F builds until this exists; it is the first product-pixel deliverable of Lane 3.

## The Problem

The choreography demo's whole value is *stunning* — and stunning is a design outcome, not an implementation afterthought. Without the artifact: scene composition, motion language, visual identity, and the recording frame all get improvised per-leaf, which is exactly how "uber brittle" surfaces happen (tonight's operator verdict on the accreted agentos views). The artifact is also the review surface: peers + the operator can falsify "stunning" on a static mock in minutes, before any engine work is spent.

## The Architectural Reality

- House precedent + bar: `apps/agentos/design/fleet-manager-cockpit-plan.html` (self-contained HTML, inline CSS, mock-first sections: product direction → honest state → lanes). This artifact follows the same shape for the demo.
- Visual coherence constraint (post-veto): the demo shares design DNA with the agentos FM module — consume the same token vocabulary the module's token floor establishes (#14578's relocated home), so the public demo and the product look like one family.
- Content constraints: shipped dockZone.v1 ops only (the eight — split/tab/auto-hide/resize choreography); scenes must be NL-drivable (every beat maps to an `execute_dock_operation` sequence the tour-runner replays); public-safe (no client names, #13032 no-perf-claims).
- Placement: `apps/agentos/design/dock-choreography-demo.html` (sibling of the cockpit plan — same dir, same review path).

## The Fix

One self-contained HTML design artifact containing:
1. **The demo's product framing** (what a stranger should feel in 30 seconds; the YouTube thumbnail moment named).
2. **Scene storyboard** — 3 scenes (split choreography / tab dance / auto-hide wave), each with: layout states before/after, the operation sequence (exact dockZone.v1 descriptors), motion notes (duration/easing classes, FLIP expectations), and the camera/recording frame.
3. **Visual identity** — token-consuming palette + type ramp; the demo chrome (stage, captions, progress affordance).
4. **The tour-mode UX** — how a viewer sees the scripted run (scene titles, operation captions, pace), and the manual-drive affordance.
5. **Honest-state section** — what the mock promises vs what shipped ops deliver today (no vaporware frames).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Artifact committed at the named path, self-contained (no external assets), renders in a bare browser.
- [ ] All 3 scenes storyboarded with exact operation descriptor sequences (copy-pasteable into the tour-runner).
- [ ] Token vocabulary consumed from the FM module floor (or explicitly stubbed pending #14578's relocation, with the mapping noted).
- [ ] Design review: ≥1 peer review against the cockpit-plan bar + operator eyes invited (the "stunning" falsifier is human).
- [ ] Every scene beat is NL-expressible (an `execute_dock_operation`/topology-read sequence exists for it — reviewer-checkable against the #14587 vocabulary).

## Out of Scope

- Demo implementation (F2–F8 leaves), the tour-runner (A4), recording (F7).
- Any new dock capability — shipped ops only.

## Related

Parent: #13158 (tree line F1) · consumed by #14589 (Demo A) + #14591 (tour replay spec) · siblings: A4 (tour-runner), #14578 (token floor, post-veto home) · bar: the cockpit plan SSOT.
